signature: Branching random walks / additive martingales on deterministic inhomogeneous trees; front positions and level-set counts ("number of particles beyond na"); multitype BRW exact exponents as Perron roots of tilted mean matrices; derivative-martingale front corrections. Instantiation: the backward Collatz tree as a multitype BRW with types = residues mod 3^k (deterministic offspring), edge displacements log2 and -log3; counting integers <= x that reach a = level-set count N(log x) for this BRW; question: does this technology reproduce/explain/exceed the Krasikov-Lagarias x^0.84 bound. ANSWER FOUND AND COMPUTATIONALLY CONFIRMED: KL's exponents ARE exactly nonlinear (min-type) Perron roots of tilted mod-3^k transition systems — reproduced their entire published table (k=2..11) to 1e-6 with a 40-line power iteration, and extended it: gamma_12=0.8531363, gamma_13=0.8630058, gamma_14=0.8724524 (their record was gamma_11=0.8417566). The annealed version (mean instead of min over the lost 3-adic digit) gives lambda=2, i.e. exponent exactly 1, at every k tested — the whole 0.84-vs-1 gap is quantified as 'adversarial vs average 3-adic refinement'.

finds.ref: J.D. Biggins, Spreading speeds in reducible multitype branching random walk, Annals of Applied Probability 22(5):1778-1821, 2012 (arXiv:1003.4716)
finds.the_theorem: For a multitype BRW whose type space decomposes into irreducible classes C_1 < ... < C_K with mean reproduction-displacement transforms m_ij(theta) = E[sum over type-j children of e^{theta z}], each irreducible class having Perron-Frobenius exponent exp(kappa_I(theta)) (hypotheses: transforms finite on an open theta-interval, initial class supercritical and primitive): the rightmost nth-generation particle satisfies B_n/n -> Gamma a.s. on survival, with Gamma = max over chains of classes of inf_theta of an explicit combination of the classes' kappa's (Thm 1.1, formula (1.4)); moreover log(number of nth-generation particles in (-infty, na]) / n converges, governed by the concave conjugates kappa* (his r-functions), and speed = Gamma(kappa*) = inf{a : kappa*(a) > 0}. Reducibility permitted; anomalous 'super-speed' characterized. Full text greps 0 for Collatz/3x+1.
finds.the_match: The backward Collatz tree with types = residues mod 3^k (classes m ≡ 2 mod 3 after KL's reduction phi^m(y)=phi^{2m}(y-1)) is a multitype BRW with DETERMINISTIC offspring: displacement -2 (child 4m) always, plus displacement alpha-2 (m≡2 mod 9, child (4m-2)/3) or alpha-1 (m≡8 mod 9, child (2m-1)/3), alpha=log2(3), in log_2-value units. The type graph is reducible exactly as in this paper: irreducible core on classes coprime to 3, plus the degenerate absorbing class ≡0 mod 3 (pure doubling, zero entropy). GAPS: (a) the mod-3^k type does NOT determine the /3-child's type — it is known only mod 3^{k-1}, so the type system is a 3-fold-refinement approximation, not an autonomous Markov chain; rigorous lower bounds require min over the 3 refinements (nonlinear, outside this paper's linear PF setting); (b) Biggins' a.s. statements concern random reproduction — for our deterministic tree only the expectation-level (Perron root / pressure) formulas transfer, which is exactly what is needed for counting.
finds.transfer: Number of depth-n tree vertices with log-value <= na grows like exp(n kappa*(a)); optimizing over n gives the count-below-x exponent gamma = the delta solving rho(M(delta)) = 1 for the tilted matrix M(delta)_{m,m'} = sum over edges m->m' of 2^{-delta d}. CONFIRMED COMPUTATIONALLY: with the min-refinement (adversarial) version this reproduces KL's lambda_k table k=2..11 to 1e-6 and extends it to k=12,13,14 (gamma = 0.8531, 0.8630, 0.8725). Solver saved at /Users/simon/Desktop/COLLATZ/kl_perron_solver.py.

finds.ref: J.D. Biggins and A. Rahimzadeh Sani, Convergence results on multitype, multivariate branching random walks, Advances in Applied Probability 37(3):681-705, 2005
finds.the_theorem: Multitype BRW on R^d; mean transform matrix M(theta)_{ij} = E_i[sum over type-j children e^{theta z}]; develops Perron-Frobenius theory for matrices that are smooth functions of complex theta and nonnegative for real theta (Perron root rho(theta), left/right eigenvectors u,v), proves uniform convergence on compacta of the normalized additive martingales W_n(theta) = sum_particles u_{type}(theta) e^{theta pos} / rho(theta)^n (mean/a.s. under x log x conditions), and obtains saddlepoint approximations and a refined large deviation theorem for numbers of particles of each type near position na. This is THE exact multitype front-position formula requested: front/level exponents are Legendre transforms of log rho(theta).
finds.the_match: Our tilted matrices M_k(lambda) on classes mod 3^k have entries lambda^{-2}, lambda^{alpha-2}, lambda^{alpha-1} placed by the deterministic transitions above — a special (deterministic, sparse: <=2 nonzeros per row) case of their setup. GAP: their theory is linear PF; the rigorous Collatz lower bound needs the min over 3-adic refinements (nonlinear PF), and their martingale limits (analogues of W_infty) have no proven meaning for the non-Markov true tree; only the eigenvalue/exponent layer transfers cleanly.
finds.transfer: Compute delta_k = root of rho(M_k(delta)) = 1 for the annealed (exact-split-averaged) system: computed, gives lambda = 2 exactly (exponent 1) at every k tested — i.e. the annealed multitype front formula says positive-exponent-1 counting, and the entire deficit 0.84->1 is the min-vs-mean gap of the unknown 3-adic digit. Also: their W_n(theta) for the true tree at theta = -delta is the natural candidate 'Collatz additive martingale' whose uniform positivity would upgrade lower bounds — a precise new object to study.

finds.ref: S.P. Lalley, Renewal theorems in symbolic dynamics, with applications to geodesic flows, noneuclidean tessellations and their fractal limits, Acta Mathematica 163:1-55, 1989
finds.the_theorem: For a topologically mixing subshift of finite type with Holder potential f that is 'eventually positive' (some iterate has uniformly positive Birkhoff sums), and nonlattice f: counting functions of the form N(t) = sum over n, over length-n paths/preimages, of g(x) 1{S_n f(x) <= t} satisfy exact asymptotics N(t) ~ C e^{delta t}, where delta is the unique root of the pressure equation P(-delta f) = 0. In matrix terms for locally constant f: spectral radius of the tilted transfer matrix equals 1. Lattice case gets oscillatory analogues.
finds.the_match: The mod-3^k Collatz backward tree is literally a finite directed graph (SFT) with locally constant displacement potential; counting tree vertices with value <= x is N(log x). HYPOTHESES CHECK: (i) eventually positive — SATISFIED, nontrivially: no two consecutive /3-edges exist in the backward tree (the (n-1)/3 child of n ≡ 4 mod 6 is always odd), so every 2-step window has displacement sum >= log(4/3) > 0; (ii) nonlattice — SATISFIED since log2/log3 irrational. GAPS: (a) Lalley counts SFT paths; the finite-type projection of the true tree overcounts (distinct paths, same integer constraints lost) — direction of bounds must be tracked, min-refinement fixes the lower-bound direction; (b) mixing on the coprime-to-3 core needs checking per k (the class ≡0 mod 3 must be excised).
finds.transfer: Upgrade the exponent statements to exact first-order asymptotics N(log x) ~ C x^{delta_k} for the mod-3^k approximants (constants included, no lattice oscillation), putting the Krasikov-Lagarias difference-inequality method inside standard thermodynamic formalism: their lambda_k is a pressure root, their C_k^max spread is the eigenfunction oscillation, and the k->infty limit question becomes spectral theory of a transfer operator on the 3-adic integers Z_3 driven by the ergodic isometry x -> 2x.

finds.ref: I. Grama, S. Mentemeier, H. Xiao, Spinal decomposition, martingale convergence and the Seneta-Heyde scaling for matrix branching random walks, arXiv:2507.09737, 2025
finds.the_theorem: For branching random walks whose particles carry products of nonnegative matrices (matrix BRW = multitype BRW with matrix weights): an analogue of Biggins' martingale convergence theorem, a spinal decomposition theorem, convergence of the derivative martingale D_n, and the Seneta-Heyde scaling c sqrt(n) W_n -> D_infty a.s. in the boundary (critical) case, built on renewal theory and duality for centered Markov random walks. Full text greps 0 for Collatz/3x+1.
finds.the_match: This is the modern second-order front machinery (spine + derivative martingale) in exactly the multitype/matrix-weight generality our mod-3^k systems live in. GAPS: substantial — (a) our tree is deterministic, theirs random: which statements survive is unclear; (b) relevance requires the Collatz multitype system to be at or approaching the BOUNDARY case, which is exactly the situation if lambda_k -> 2 (annealed value = 2 exactly, computed): the k->infty system is conjecturally critical; (c) derivative martingales correct front positions by (3/2) log n — they never move first-order exponents, so this cannot by itself push past 0.84.
finds.transfer: Hot program: if lambda_k -> 2, the true counting is the boundary case and the Seneta-Heyde/derivative-martingale layer predicts the correction: pi_a(x) = x / polylog(x)-type asymptotics rather than x^{1-epsilon}. Concretely: build the spine measure (size-biased backward Collatz walk = random walk with log2/-log3 steps tilted to zero drift, i.e. the (1/phi)-flavored tiltings already seen in cryptid heuristics) and compute whether the derivative-martingale limit D_infty makes sense for the deterministic tree.

finds.ref: J.D. Biggins, B.D. Lubachevsky, A. Shwartz, A. Weiss, A branching random walk with a barrier, Annals of Applied Probability 1(4):573-581, 1991
finds.the_theorem: BRW in which particles falling on the wrong side of a linearly moving barrier are killed (motivation: rollback cascades in parallel discrete-event simulation — an OR/simulation venue). Dichotomy theorem: with b = mean viable offspring and barrier slope vs displacement rates, survival of the barrier-constrained population and its exponential growth rate are determined by the sign of the tilted exponent (survives iff b > e^h criterion, growth rate = kappa*(a) analogue); at criticality extinction is a.s. (NOTE: blindness verified from abstract/secondary sources only — Project Euclid blocked the full-text download; mentions of Collatz in a 1991 OR-motivated probability paper are implausible but unverified.)
finds.the_match: KL's phi functions are DEFINED via pi*_a(x) = integers n <= x reaching a with ALL intermediate iterates <= x — i.e. the count of tree rays confined below a barrier, exactly a barrier-constrained BRW count. Also matches the divergence-certificate side: an invariant set avoiding 1 with all orbits above a growing barrier is a killed-BRW survival event; and Antihydra's 'counter hits exact boundary against positive drift' is the killed-walk-at-barrier event whose probability the (1/phi)^n heuristic estimates. GAPS: KL's barrier is constant-in-generation at level x (not linear); Antihydra is a single walk (no branching) with an exact-hitting (not one-sided-killing) condition.
finds.transfer: Prove that the barrier-constrained exponent equals the unconstrained one for the Collatz multitype system (KL implicitly need pi* ~ pi at the exponent level): the barrier dichotomy gives the tool — the optimal tilted path from a to 2^y a stays below the barrier iff the optimizing slope is positive, which holds here; this would justify replacing pi* by pi with no exponent loss for all k.

finds.ref: S. Gaubert and J. Gunawardena, The Perron-Frobenius theorem for homogeneous, monotone functions, Transactions of the AMS 356(12):4931-4950, 2004
finds.the_theorem: For F: R^n_{>0} -> R^n_{>0} monotone and homogeneous of degree 1 (e.g. any min/max of linear maps with nonnegative coefficients): a nonlinear spectral radius exists, characterized by the Collatz-Wielandt formula r(F) = inf over x>0 of max_i F(x)_i/x_i; existence of positive eigenvectors is controlled by combinatorics of an associated digraph; nonlinear power iteration and policy iteration compute r(F) with certified two-sided Collatz-Wielandt bounds. (Venue: tropical/control theory; the Collatz here is Lothar Collatz himself, 1942 — the same person, in a paper otherwise unrelated to 3x+1.)
finds.the_match: KL's 'sup lambda such that the LP L_k(lambda) has a positive feasible solution' is EXACTLY the statement r(F_lambda) <= 1 where F_lambda is the concave monotone homogeneous map (min over the 3 lost-3-adic-digit refinements of tilted linear maps) — I implemented this and reproduced their entire Table 2 (k=2..11, lambda to 1e-6) and their believed-optimality claims (their conditions (1),(2)). Since F_lambda is a min of linear maps, policy iteration expresses lambda_k as a MINIMUM over policies of ordinary Perron roots — a 'lower spectral radius over policies', connecting to the JSR/control literature already in the repo. No gaps at finite k: this is an exact reformulation, verified numerically.
finds.transfer: Certified computation of lambda_k for k = 15..18 on 32 cores (states = 2/3 * 3^k <= 8.6e7 at k=18, <= 2 sparse terms per state; Collatz-Wielandt gives rigorous upper AND lower certificates; policy iteration for exactness). Already computed here through k=14: gamma_12 = 0.8531363, gamma_13 = 0.8630058, gamma_14 = 0.8724524 — past the published 0.8417566.

best_single_bet: The lambda_k computation, because it is already yielding numbers past the 20-year-old record. Status: I reconstructed Krasikov-Lagarias's difference-inequality systems I_k directly from their Prop 2.1 as nonlinear (min-type) Perron eigenproblems on the 2/3 * 3^{k-1} classes m ≡ 2 mod 3 mod 3^k, reproduced their entire published exponent table k=2..11 to 1e-6 (solver: /Users/simon/Desktop/COLLATZ/kl_perron_solver.py), and computed NEW values gamma_12 = 0.8531363, gamma_13 = 0.8630058, gamma_14 = 0.8724524; the annealed (mean-over-refinements) system gives lambda = 2 (exponent exactly 1) at every k. First move of the week: (1) verify the k=12..14 eigenvectors are feasible points of KL's retarded LP L^NT_k(lambda) (their Theorem 2.2 then makes pi_a(x) >= x^0.8724 a THEOREM — an unconditional improvement of the best known lower bound; use interval arithmetic, and their conditions (1),(2) held for all k<=11 so feasibility is expected); (2) in parallel, run k = 15..18 on the 32-core machine with policy iteration + Collatz-Wielandt certificates and fit the increment decay of gamma_k to decide empirically whether lambda_k -> 2 (KL: lambda_k -> 2 iff pi_1(x) >= x^{1-eps} for all eps) or stalls at lambda_infty < 2 — either outcome is a publishable structural fact about the limit of the entire difference-inequality method, and the k->infty object (min-type transfer operator on Z_3 driven by the ergodic isometry x -> 2x, annealed value exactly 2) is a clean new attack surface: proving equidistribution of the refinement split fast enough would give the x^{1-eps} bound.
dead_ends: (1) Single-type BRW models of the backward tree fail badly: the naive one-type mean transform m(theta) = 2^theta + (1/3)3^{-theta} has min > 1, predicting infinitely many vertices below any level — the no-two-consecutive-(n-1)/3 constraint (multitype structure) is essential, so Biggins 1977 one-type Chernoff theory alone cannot reproduce KL; don't pursue one-type models. (2) Heavy-tailed multitype BRW extremes (arXiv:2509.14808, 'heaviest tail wins') — irrelevant: our displacements take two bounded values. (3) Generic searches for 'number of particles above a linear boundary' mostly return killing-at-origin/random-environment variants (catalytic BRWs, absorbing sources — MDPI/Yarovaya school), not deterministic-tree level counts; not useful. (4) Classical Crump-Mode-Jagers/Nerman Malthusian-parameter theory (counting with characteristics, N(t) ~ C e^{alpha t} with alpha the Perron root of the Laplace-transform matrix) is blocked by a hypothesis failure: it requires children born AFTER parents, but the /3-edge has displacement -log3 (children 'born earlier'); would need two-sided Markov renewal theory instead — the Lalley/pressure route avoids this cleanly since 2-step windows are uniformly positive. (5) Derivative martingales / Aidekon-Shi corrections cannot move the first-order exponent past 0.84 — they only matter if the system is at the boundary case (relevant only for the endgame x/polylog question). (6) Project Euclid blocks direct curl of PDFs (Lalley 1989 and Biggins-Lubachevsky-Shwartz-Weiss 1991 not saved locally; HTML shells deleted); full-text Collatz-blindness of BLSW 1991 unverified for that reason. (7) Biggins' Sheffield staff page (biggins.staff.shef.ac.uk) is DNS-dead; Biggins-Sani 2005 PDF only behind Cambridge paywall — blindness verified from abstract.
